subject: Project Management
title: Lead Time and Cycle Time
description: Learn Lead Time and Cycle Time in one minute.

hook.text: Lead time is the customer's clock: how long from request to delivery?

buildup.text: Cycle time is the team's clock: how long from starting work to finishing it?

discovery.text: The gap between lead time and cycle time is pure waiting — the waste Kanban targets.

twist.text: Most managers track output (how much). Kanban tracks flow (how fast). They're different questions.

climax.text: Predictability matters more than speed. Consistent cycle times build trust with stakeholders.

punchline.text: Customers don't want fast. They want predictable.

quiz.question: What is the difference between lead time and cycle time?
options: Lead time is request to delivery; cycle time is start to finish of work
Lead time is always shorter than cycle time
